text.title: compaction-preserve-refs Output Contract
text.summary: YAML schema for the compacted state. Six required keys, no free-text summary, drift-resistant.

example.code: goal: refactor auth module to use refresh-token rotation files_touched: - src/auth/login.py:42 - src/auth/jwt.py:88-110 decisions: - chose RS256 over HS256 (ADR-007) - rotation interval = 15 min errors_open: - "test_login_invalid_pw fails at AssertionError: expected 401 got 200" refs: - {kind: pr, id: "1842"} - {kind: log, id: ".agent/results/pytest_4882.log"} next_action: implement refresh-token rotation in jwt.py
example.note: Every key fillable from the conversation. Resume by grep on `goal` and reading `files_touched`.

example.code: summary: | Worked on auth, made some progress, will continue next session. status: in-progress
example.note: No required keys, no references, no actionable `next_action`. Next session restarts from zero; the validator rejects this file.

list.item: Deduplicate identical entries within any list.
Top-level keys are exactly the six required keys, no others.
Every list element is present and non-empty when the conversation contained matching data.
Empty lists are `[]`, never the string "none".
Each `refs` entry has both `kind` (from enum) and `id` fields.
